descriptions: The Hogan Gang had a close business partnership with the Russo Gang.

descriptions: After the alliance broke, the Pillow Gang fought the Russo Gang.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1931, Fresina was murdered and members of the Pillow gang continued fighting with the Russo gang and the Green Ones.

descriptions: The Pillow Gang allied with the Russo Gang to battle the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: Fresina formed an alliance with splinter group of the Green Ones called the Russo gang led by Tony Russo.

descriptions: The Russo Gang battled the Green Ones.
edges.evidence_quote: The two gangs battled with the Green Ones until 1928, when the Russo gang lost too many members and blamed the Pillow gang for betraying them.

descriptions: The Russo Gang continued fighting the Green Ones after 1928.
edges.evidence_quote: In 1931, Fresina was murdered and members of the Pillow gang continued fighting with the Russo gang and the Green Ones.

Four Corner Hustlers.description: The Four Corner Hustlers is an African American street gang founded in the West Garfield Park neighborhood of Chicago in the late 1960s by Walter Wheat and Freddy Gauge. The gang is known for being one of the most violent and feared on Chicago's West Side. It is allied with the People Nation and has a long-standing rivalry with the Folk Nation. The gang is involved in various criminal activities including drug trafficking, robbery, and murder.

Blue Angels Motorcycle Club.description: International outlaw motorcycle club formed in Glasgow, Scotland in 1963 by Allan Morrison and Billy Gordon. It is the oldest outlaw biker club in Europe and one of the largest in the UK, with chapters in Scotland, England, Belgium, Spain, and Germany. The club has been linked to organized crime and designated a criminal motorcycle gang by Belgian Federal Police.

descriptions: Blue Angels were listed alongside Bandidos as criminal motorcycle gangs in Belgium, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Belgium's Federal Police named the Blue Angels as one of four criminal motorcycle gangs operating in the country, along with the Bandidos, Hells Angels and Outlaws.

descriptions: Members of Garthamlock Blackhawks joined the Blue Angels after gang wars ended.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Roadburners and Garthamlock Blackhawks gangs subsequently joined the Blue Angels.

descriptions: Blue Angels were rivals of the English Hells Angels.
edges.evidence_quote: rivalling the English Hells Angels

descriptions: Blue Angels fought the Hells Angels in the 1970s.
edges.evidence_quote: We fought the Hells Angels in the 1970s which is why they don't have a chapter here.

descriptions: Blue Angels had a feud with the Mad Dogs Motorcycle Club.
edges.evidence_quote: A feud developed between the Blue Angels and the Mad Dogs Motorcycle Club after senior Blue Angels member Daniel Laponder broke away and formed the Mad Dogs in 2023.

descriptions: Blue Angels members forced a Mongrel Mob member to surrender his colors.
edges.evidence_quote: Four Blue Angels members ... pleaded guilty to threatening behaviour relating to an incident ... in which they forced a member of the Mongrel Mob to hand over his 'colours' at his home

descriptions: Blue Angels were in a turf war with the Nomads.
edges.evidence_quote: A turf war involving the Blue Angels and the Nomads – a motorcycle club formed in Aberdeen in 1966 – started after the Blue Angels expanded into Aberdeenshire

descriptions: Blue Angels opposed Outlaws recruiting in Scotland, indicating conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Charlie Allan was hospitalised after he was allegedly beaten ... by two members of the Blue Angels who accused him of assisting the Outlaws in founding a chapter in Dundee the previous year. The Blue Angels were involved in an effort to stop the Outlaws recruiting in Scotland at the time.

descriptions: Blue Angels were listed alongside Outlaws as criminal motorcycle gangs in Belgium, but no direct relationship is stated.
edges.evidence_quote: Belgium's Federal Police named the Blue Angels as one of four criminal motorcycle gangs operating in the country, along with the Bandidos, Hells Angels and Outlaws.

descriptions: Blue Angels formed an alliance with the Road Rats of London.
edges.evidence_quote: The Blue Angels began associating with motorcycle clubs in England during the 1970s, forming an alliance with the Road Rats of London

descriptions: Members of Roadburners joined the Blue Angels after gang wars ended.
edges.evidence_quote: Members of the Roadburners and Garthamlock Blackhawks gangs subsequently joined the Blue Angels.

descriptions: Blue Angels were in a biker war with the Satans Slaves.
edges.evidence_quote: The Blue Angels were reportedly involved in a biker war with the Tayside chapter of the Satans Slaves in 2011.

descriptions: The Tribe is a support club of the Blue Angels MC.
edges.evidence_quote: The Tribe, the BAMC's support club, was formed in Edinburgh in November 2011

Temple Street gang.description: Street gang based in downtown Los Angeles, founded in 1923 by Filipino and Mexican youths. Involved in murders, assaults, burglaries, drug trafficking, and gun trafficking. Colors are blue and black. Active in Central Los Angeles and the Philippines.

Syrian Democratic Forces.description: The Syrian Democratic Forces (SDF) is a Kurdish-led coalition of ethnic militias and rebel groups founded on 10 October 2015. It serves as the official military wing of the Democratic Autonomous Administration of North and East Syria (Rojava). The SDF aims to create a secular, democratic, and federalized Syria, and has been a key U.S. ally in the fight against the Islamic State. Since 2025, it has been integrating into Syrian state institutions.

Grey Wolves.description: The Grey Wolves are a Turkish far-right ultranationalist and neo-fascist political movement, officially the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP). Founded by Colonel Alparslan Türkeş in the late 1960s, they were heavily involved in political violence in Turkey in the 1970s, including the Maraş massacre. They have been designated as a terrorist organization by Kazakhstan and banned in Azerbaijan and France. The group is active in Turkey, Northern Cyprus, Western Europe, and post-Soviet states, and is suspected of ties to the Turkish deep state and organized crime.

descriptions: Alperen Hearths is listed as an ally of the Grey Wolves in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths

descriptions: Alperen Hearths are listed as allies of the Grey Wolves in the infobox.
edges.evidence_quote: Allies: Alperen Hearths

edges.target: Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia
descriptions: Grey Wolves members targeted ASALA members.
edges.evidence_quote: They mostly targeted members of the Armenian Secret Army for the Liberation of Armenia (ASALA), which attacked Turkish embassies abroad in retaliation for the Turkish state's continued denial of the Armenian genocide.

descriptions: The Army of Conquest allegedly conspired with the Grey Wolves in an assassination.
edges.evidence_quote: Youm7, an Egyptian news site picked up a document allegedly issued by the Army of Conquest (Jaish al-Fatah), which claimed that it conspired with the Turkistan Islamic Party and the Grey Wolves in the December 2016 assassination of Andrei Karlov, the Russian ambassador to Turkey.

descriptions: The Grey Wolves are suspected of having close dealings with the Counter-Guerrilla.
edges.evidence_quote: The organization has long been a prominent suspect in investigations into the deep state in Turkey, and is suspected of having close dealings in the past with the Counter-Guerrilla, the Turkish branch of the NATO Operation Gladio, as well as the Turkish mafia.

descriptions: Grey Wolves members affiliated with the Great Union Party (BBP) supported Chechen separatists.
edges.evidence_quote: the Grey Wolves with most pro-Chechen stance were those affiliated with the Islamist Great Union Party (BBP), which had split from MHP in 1993.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization

edges.evidence_quote: The Grey Wolves ... is a terrorist Turkish far-right political movement and the youth wing of the Nationalist Movement Party (MHP).

edges.evidence_quote: From 2014-2018 the Osmanen Germania were an extreme right criminal gang working as security guards for the gray wolves.

edges.evidence_quote: Opponents: Islamic State, Kurdistan Workers' Party, Kurdish Hezbollah, Raiders Organization

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva fought a bloody skirmish with Velikolukskaya after splitting from them.
edges.evidence_quote: A bloody skirmish with the Velikolukskaya gang caused Kumarin to fall into a month-long coma.

descriptions: Tambovskaya Bratva merged into Velikolukskaya for protection and racketeering operations.
edges.evidence_quote: the gang merged into the large criminal group Velikolukskaya (from Velikie Luki, Pskov region) and was engaged mostly in commercial racketeering.

descriptions: Năm Cam planned to kill Tài chém, leading to a chase by Tài chém's gang.
edges.evidence_quote: Năm Cam, under the guidance of Bảy Sy planned to kill Tài chém, a notorious gangster in the first district of Saigon, but the plan failed and resulted in Năm Cam being chased by Tài chém's gang brothers.

descriptions: Corsican crime bosses collaborated with the Milice.

edges.evidence_quote: Like Independent Soldiers , Indian Posse , United Nations , Bo-Gars, Native Syndicate , and Crazy Dragons, NPF has an interprovincial presence.

descriptions: The Maesano family was part of a broader criminal association that included the Verno family.

descriptions: TCC was created by former PCC leaders as a splinter group, leading to conflict.
edges.evidence_quote: Marcola would eventually order the deaths of Geleião and Cesinha for having testified to the police and for creating the Terceiro Comando da Capital (Third Capital Command, TCC).

Aryan Republican Army.description: White nationalist terrorist gang that robbed 22 banks in the Midwest from 1994 to 1996. Founded in 1992 by Donna Langan and Richard Lee Guthrie Jr. The group stockpiled weapons for a race war and allegedly conspired with Timothy McVeigh before the Oklahoma City bombing. Members were arrested in 1996 after informants cooperated with the FBI.

descriptions: ARA members had links to Aryan Nations; some were members.
edges.evidence_quote: Members, who had links to Neo-Nazism and white supremacism

descriptions: ARA leader Langan was ordained at a Klan-affiliated church.
edges.evidence_quote: Langan converted to Mormonism and became an ordained minister at what authorities describe as a Ku Klux Klan-affiliated church.

descriptions: Salakau routinely attacked 18 Sio Yi Ho.
edges.evidence_quote: Attacks on rival gangs such as Sakongsa, Omega and 18 Sio Yi Ho were somewhat of a routine occurrence.

descriptions: The Costantino were part of the Gallico faction in the feud.
edges.evidence_quote: Per la prima fazione erano presenti anche i Frisina, i Mazzullo, i, Gramuglia e i Costantino

Puro Tango Blast.description: Puro Tango Blast is a loose affiliation of regionally based Hispanic street and prison gangs from major Texas cities, formed in the 1980s for protection against established prison gangs. Unlike traditional prison gangs, it lacks a strict hierarchy and has relaxed membership requirements. Members identify with hometown cliques called Tangos, using sports team logos or area codes as tattoos. The organization is involved in drug trafficking, robbery, and other crimes.

descriptions: The text states that Mexican drug cartels employ these gangs to distribute drugs, indicating a cooperative relationship.
edges.evidence_quote: Among these gangs, that range from street gangs to prison gangs, are the Texas Syndicate, the Latin Kings, the Mexican Mafia, Puro Tango Blast (Vallucos), the Hermandad de Pistoleros Latinos, and the Tri-City Bombers—all based in the Rio Grande Valley and Webb County, Texas.

edges.evidence_quote: Incarcerated Hispanic men from major Texas cities (including Houston , Dallas , Fort Worth , Austin ), have banded together for protection from established security threat groups like Mexikanemi and the Texas Syndicate .

Bloody Tubs.description: A 19th-century gang of street thugs based in Baltimore and Philadelphia, formed in the mid-1850s. They worked for Nativist Know Nothing politicians to commit election fraud, using violent tactics such as blocking voting booths and attacking opponents. The gang was broken up by police after 1870 due to public outrage.

descriptions: Bloody Tubs were rivals of Baltimore Irish gangs.
edges.evidence_quote: Rivals: Baltimore Irish gangs

Cannonball Motorcycle Club.description: Outlaw motorcycle club based in Finland and Estonia, founded in Helsinki in 1991 by Jari Uotila. Designated as an organized crime group by the National Bureau of Investigation. Operates eleven chapters and a sub-division, Squad 32, with over 200 members. Briefly disbanded in 2017 due to internal dispute but resolved and continued operations.

descriptions: Cannonball MC, allied with Hells Angels, was attacked by Bandidos (Undertakers).
edges.evidence_quote: The vice-president of Cannonball MC , a club allied with the Hells Angels, was wounded by an Undertakers member

descriptions: Cannonball members were involved in a shootout with Chosen Ones MC in 2020.
edges.evidence_quote: Nine motorcycle gang members were charged with various crimes, including attempted murder, after Cannonball members were involved in a shootout with rival bikers at the clubhouse of the Chosen Ones MC in Joensuu on 22 August 2020.

descriptions: Cannonball MC was a support club for Hells Angels from 1991 to 1996.
edges.evidence_quote: During the initial five years of its existence, Cannonball MC operated as a support club of the Hells Angels.

descriptions: Moraine MC was allied with Chosen Ones MC in the shootout against Cannonball.
edges.evidence_quote: Four members of the Chosen Ones and the allied Moraine MC were also charged in the case.

descriptions: Cannonball and Overkill MC were aligned against Undertakers MC during the Nordic Biker War.
edges.evidence_quote: As a Hells Angels support club, Cannonball was aligned with the HA prospect chapter Overkill MC against the Undertakers MC, a prospect club of the Bandidos, during the Nordic Biker War in the 1990s.

descriptions: Rogues Gallery was founded by former members of Cannonball's Lahti chapter.
edges.evidence_quote: The former members of this chapter then founded the Rogues Gallery gang in the city in January 2000.

descriptions: Squad 32 is a sub-division of Cannonball MC.
edges.evidence_quote: Additionally, Cannonball's sub-division, Squad 32, has six branches.

descriptions: Cannonball was in conflict with Undertakers MC during the Nordic Biker War.
edges.evidence_quote: As a Hells Angels support club, Cannonball was aligned with the HA prospect chapter Overkill MC against the Undertakers MC, a prospect club of the Bandidos, during the Nordic Biker War in the 1990s.

Sombra Negra.description: Death squad based in El Salvador, composed mainly of police and military personnel acting as vigilantes targeting criminals and gang members. Active since around 1994, it was dismantled in 1996 but resurfaced in 2014. Known for targeting Mara Salvatrucha and Mara 18, and for executing victims with gunshots to the back of the head.

descriptions: Sombra Negra hunted maras, including 18th Street.
edges.evidence_quote: Maras were hunted by death squads including Sombra Negra.

descriptions: Barrio 18 is an enemy of Sombra Negra.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Sombra Negra

descriptions: Sombra Negra hunted maras, including Mara Salvatrucha.
edges.evidence_quote: Maras were hunted by death squads including Sombra Negra.

descriptions: Infobox lists Sombra Negra as an enemy of MS-13.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Mara Barrio 18 , Nayib Bukele , Gobierno salvadoreño , Pandilla Mao Mao , Gobierno federal de los Estados Unidos , Cártel de Juárez , Los Negros , Sombra Negra , Cártel de Tijuana , Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva , Bloods , Norteños , Armenian Power , Latin Kings y Los Del Sur

descriptions: Infobox lists Sombra Negra as enemy of Mara Salvatrucha.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: Mara Barrio 18 , Nayib Bukele , Gobierno salvadoreño , Pandilla Mao Mao , Gobierno federal de los Estados Unidos , Cártel de Juárez , Los Negros , Sombra Negra , Cártel de Tijuana , Cártel de los Beltrán Leyva , Bloods , Norteños , Armenian Power , Latin Kings y Los Del Sur

descriptions: Sombra Negra targets and executes members of Mara 18.
edges.evidence_quote: Apuntando a la Mara Salvatrucha y a la Mara 18

descriptions: Sombra Negra considered FMLN an enemy during the civil war.
edges.evidence_quote: Enemigos: FMLN ( Durante la Guerra Civil )

descriptions: Sombra Negra targets and executes members of Mara Salvatrucha.
edges.evidence_quote: Apuntando a la Mara Salvatrucha y a la Mara 18
